req.title: Bulk import employees — variant 5
req.statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers bulk CSV import with per-row validation, partial commit semantics, 2FA confirmation, and explicit error codes for wrong file type (415) and missing headers (422). I derived five test cases: (1) EP positive — all valid rows imported after 2FA; (2) NEG — non-CSV file triggers 415; (3) NEG — CSV missing required headers triggers 422; (4) DT — mixed CSV where only valid rows are committed and an error report lists invalid rows; (5) BVA — single-row CSV (minimum non-empty payload) is committed correctly. Together these cover the normal path, both documented error paths, partial-commit logic, and the boundary of minimal input.

tests.name: test_bulk_import_all_valid_rows_committed_after_2fa
preconditions: 2FA is enabled for the hr-admin account
CSV file contains only valid employee rows with all required headers
actions: POST /employees/import with a well-formed CSV multipart payload
Assert HTTP 200 and parse response JSON
expected: imported_count equals total rows in CSV
Each employee record exists in the system after import
tests.executable:
import pytest
import csv, io

def test_bulk_import_all_valid_rows_committed_after_2fa(
    hr_admin_session,   # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session
    totp_code,          # <fixture>: current TOTP token for hr-admin
    base_url,           # <fixture>: app root URL
    cleanup_imported_employees,  # <fixture>: deletes test employees after test
):
    rows = [
        {"first_name": "Alice", "last_name": "Smith", "email": "alice@example.com", "department": "Eng"},
        {"first_name": "Bob",   "last_name": "Jones", "email": "bob@example.com",   "department": "HR"},
    ]
    csv_bytes = <build_csv_bytes>(rows)  # <helper_fn>

    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": ("employees.csv", csv_bytes, "text/csv")},
        data={"totp": totp_code},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == len(rows)
    assert body["error_count"] == 0
    assert body["errors"] == []

tests.name: test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415
preconditions: File to upload is a plain-text file with .txt extension and text/plain MIME type
actions: POST /employees/import with a .txt file as the multipart payload
expected: Response body contains an error message referencing unsupported file type
tests.executable:
def test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415(
    hr_admin_session,
    totp_code,
    base_url,
):
    txt_content = b"first_name,last_name,email,department\nAlice,Smith,a@b.com,Eng"

    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": ("employees.txt", txt_content, "text/plain")},
        data={"totp": totp_code},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 415
    body = resp.json()
    assert "unsupported" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "media type" in body.get("error", "").lower()

tests.name: test_bulk_import_csv_missing_required_header_returns_422
preconditions: CSV file is a valid CSV but omits the required 'email' column header
actions: POST /employees/import with a CSV missing the 'email' header
expected: Response body identifies the missing required column
trace: R-WEB-101: 422 when header missing required columns
tests.executable:
def test_bulk_import_csv_missing_required_header_returns_422(
    hr_admin_session,
    totp_code,
    base_url,
):
    # 'email' column intentionally omitted
    csv_bytes = b"first_name,last_name,department\nAlice,Smith,Eng"

    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": ("employees.csv", csv_bytes, "text/csv")},
        data={"totp": totp_code},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "email" in error_text or "missing" in error_text or "required" in error_text

tests.name: test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_partial_commit_with_error_report
preconditions: CSV has 3 rows: 2 valid, 1 invalid (missing email value)
actions: Verify only valid rows were committed
Verify error report lists exactly the invalid row
expected: errors array contains entry for row 3 with a validation message
Two valid employees exist in the system
Invalid employee does not exist in the system
trace: R-WEB-101: commit only rows that pass validation
tests.executable:
def test_bulk_import_mixed_csv_partial_commit_with_error_report(
    hr_admin_session,
    totp_code,
    base_url,
    cleanup_imported_employees,  # <fixture>
):
    csv_bytes = (
        b"first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
        b"Carol,White,carol@example.com,Finance\n"
        b"Dan,Brown,dan@example.com,Legal\n"
        b"Eve,Black,,Engineering\n"  # missing email — invalid
    )

    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": ("mixed.csv", csv_bytes, "text/csv")},
        data={"totp": totp_code},
    )

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 2
    assert body["error_count"] == 1
    assert len(body["errors"]) == 1
    err = body["errors"][0]
    assert err.get("row") == 3 or "Eve" in str(err)
    assert <employee_exists>(hr_admin_session, base_url, "carol@example.com")  # <helper_fn>
    assert not <employee_exists>(hr_admin_session, base_url, "")  # <helper_fn>

tests.name: test_bulk_import_single_row_csv_boundary_minimum_payload
preconditions: CSV contains exactly one data row (minimum non-empty payload) with all required fields
actions: POST /employees/import with a CSV containing only the header row and one data row
Verify the single employee was created
expected: The single employee record exists in the system
trace: R-WEB-101: boundary — minimum data payload of one row
tests.executable:
def test_bulk_import_single_row_csv_boundary_minimum_payload(
    hr_admin_session,
    totp_code,
    base_url,
    cleanup_imported_employees,  # <fixture>
):
    csv_bytes = (
        b"first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
        b"Frank,Green,frank.green.bva@example.com,Ops\n"
    )

    resp = hr_admin_session.post(
        f"{base_url}/employees/import",
        files={"file": ("single_row.csv", csv_bytes, "text/csv")},
        data={"totp": totp_code},
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported_count"] == 1
    assert body["error_count"] == 0
    assert <employee_exists>(hr_admin_session, base_url, "frank.green.bva@example.com")  # <helper_fn>
